mechanism_narrative: A sustained federal funds rate below 0.25% compresses equity discount rates and forces portfolio rebalancing into risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0% signals severe labor market slack and depressed consumer purchasing power, which directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and generates negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and investment demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Joint negative growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary easing, a month-over-month increase in industrial production signals improving corporate output capacity and supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth when FEDFUNDS is declining or below 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 230 while policy rates remain pinned near zero, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting equity market repricing and negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 230, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and persistently high unemployment indicates labor force participation distortion, which suppresses wage growth expectations and leads to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or 60-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.0% chained dollars reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion, which directly supports corporate earnings revisions and generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0%, or the signal fails to achieve a Rank IC > 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 90.0 index points, it indicates severe capacity utilization constraints and weak manufacturing demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of months where INDPRO drops below 90.0, or the threshold signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
